name: Col d'Azet – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees
name_original: Circuit 9 : Col d’Azet — Les cols emblématiques des Pyrénées
description:
The Col d'Azet is less known and frequented than its prestigious neighbours. However, since 1997, it has already been used ten times by the Tour de France. Located in the Louron valley between Saint-Lary-Soulan and Loudenvielle, it culminates at 1,580 meters above sea level.

The start of the course is in the center of Saint-Lary-Soulan. A false downward flat begins by bringing you to the pretty mountain village of Arreau. Then you start a long false flat going up to Génos and its lake. This is where the ascent to the Col d'Azet starts.

The climb is just over seven kilometers long and includes some passages at more than 13%. This one has several switchbacks that quickly take you above the valley and its lake. The Col d'Azet culminates at 1,580 meters and therefore offers a remarkable landscape on its slopes as well as at its summit.

The descent is made on the foothills of a beautiful rocky ridge to the small town of Azet. Several switchbacks meander under the village before reaching the village of Saint-Lary-Soulan.
region_intro: Hiking around Saint-Lary-Soulan offers access to diverse Pyrenean landscapes, characterized by high-altitude lakes, mountain passes, and deep valleys. The region is situated in the Hautes-Pyrénées, providing a gateway to extensive trail networks within the Néouvielle National Nature Reserve. This protected area features over 70 lakes, including Lac d'Orédon and Lac d'Aubert, alongside unique flora and fauna. Geological formations like the Clarabide gorges and various mountain passes further define the terrain. Best hiking trails around Saint-Lary-Soulan The most popular hiking route is The Néouvielle lakes via Col d'Estoudou, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the high-altitude lakes within the Néouvielle National Nature Reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aubert and Aumar lakes, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This trail explores two of the notable lakes in the region, providing scenic mountain and water views. Local hikers also love the Néouvielle Tour, a 11.5 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through the Néouvielle National Nature Reserve, often completed in about 7 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Saint-Lary-Soulan is defined by Pyrenean landscapes, high-altitude lakes, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging multi-day treks. The routes in Saint-Lary-Soulan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 9000 hikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Lary-Soulan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What makes the hiking around Saint-Lary-Soulan unique?
A: Hiking in Saint-Lary-Soulan offers access to diverse Pyrenean landscapes, characterized by high-altitude lakes, dramatic mountain passes like Col d'Azet, and deep valleys such as the peaceful Rioumajou Valley. The region is a gateway to the extensive trail networks within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3942029/natural-monuments-around-saint-lary-soulan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Néouvielle National Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, renowned for its over 70 lakes and unique flora and fauna.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Saint-Lary-Soulan?
A: Yes, Saint-Lary-Soulan offers several easy to moderate options suitable for families. The Rioumajou Valley is known for its peaceful, family-friendly walks. While specific easy routes aren't detailed in the guide, the region has 15 easy trails out of 118 total. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the numerous lakes within the Néouvielle National Nature Reserve, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Aubert&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Oule&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cap-de-Long&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic Clarabide gorges are also a significant geological feature. Many trails offer panoramic views from mountain passes like the Col d'Azet and Hourquette d'Ancizan.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383204039/neouvielle-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Néouvielle Tour&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.5-mile (18.6 km) loop through the Néouvielle National Nature Reserve. The region research also mentions a moderate circular route of Soulan from Saint-Lary-Soulan, offering another option for a round trip.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Saint-Lary-Soulan?
A: The summer months are ideal for hiking in Saint-Lary-Soulan, offering the best conditions to enjoy the high-altitude lakes and mountain passes. The region is a prime destination for outdoor activities during this season. While winter activities like skiing are popular, specific winter hiking trails may require specialized gear and knowledge due to snow and ice.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While many areas in the Pyrenees are dog-friendly, it's important to note that the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3942029/natural-monuments-around-saint-lary-soulan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Néouvielle National Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a protected area. Regulations regarding dogs can vary within national parks and reserves, often requiring them to be on a leash or restricting access in certain zones to protect wildlife. Always check local signage or park rules before heading out with your dog.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking trails that pass through Saint-Lary-Soulan?
A: Yes, Saint-Lary-Soulan is a significant point for long-distance hikers. The famous GR10, which traverses the Pyrenees from the Atlantic to the Mediterranean, passes through the area. Additionally, sections of the HexaTrek, another long-distance trail spanning over 3,000 km across France, are found in the Western Pyrenees near Saint-Lary-Soulan.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the trails?
A: Hikers can expect breathtaking panoramic views throughout the region. Trails often lead to stunning vistas of the Pyrenean peaks, high-altitude lakes, and deep valleys. Mountain passes like the Col d'Azet and Hourquette d'Ancizan are particularly known for their expansive scenic outlooks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Saint-Lary-Soulan?
A: The routes in Saint-Lary-Soulan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse Pyrenean landscapes, the beauty of the high-altitude lakes, and the variety of trails available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there places to stay overnight or refuges along the trails?
A: Yes, the region has several huts and refuges that can serve as overnight stops or rest points. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastan Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Glère Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge de Campana de Cloutou&lt;/a&gt;. These can be particularly useful for longer or multi-day treks.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Saint-Lary-Soulan?
A: There are over 100 hiking trails around Saint-Lary-Soulan, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. Specifically, komoot lists 118 tours, with 15 easy, 66 moderate, and 37 difficult routes.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383204039/neouvielle-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Néouvielle Tour&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.5-mile (18.6 km) trail. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383115795/pic-de-neouvielle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Néouvielle&lt;/a&gt; route, which involves significant elevation gain to reach the summit.
tips: Saint-Lary-Soulan is an emblematic mountain village of the Pyrenees in the Pays d'Aure. Located in the Neste valley, it offers a wide choice of accommodation, restaurants and shops.

Charming little mountain village located on the banks of the Neste. Its location is ideal for tackling several passes in the area, including the Col d'Aspin, Peyresourde and Portet, among others.

You will find there accommodation possibilities and supplies.

Located in the Louron valley, the Col d'Azet peaks at an altitude of 1,580 meters. This passage connects the village of Loudenvielle and that of Azet on the heights of Saint-Lary-Soulan. The ascent from Lake Génos is 7.5 kilometers for an average of 8.3% with some passages at 13%.

The pretty little village of Azet is located on the heights of Saint-Lary-Soulan a few kilometers from the Col d'Azet.
Azet is ideal for taking a break and refilling cans.
